general.signupRestrictionsDescription: Control who can create new accounts when using local authentication.
general.restrictNewSignupsDescription: When enabled, no new users can register.
general.acceptOnlySpecificDomainsDescription: When domains are listed, only users with matching email addresses can register. Leave empty to allow all domains.
general.noDomainRestrictions: No domain restrictions — all email addresses are accepted.

dockerRebuild.title: Docker Image Rebuilds
dockerRebuild.noRearchEnabledDescription: Docker image rebuild scheduling is only available when at least one subresource has the ReArch integration enabled. Enable ReArch on a repository's settings to use this feature.
dockerRebuild.scheduledRebuildsDescription: Automatically rebuild all Docker images for subresources with ReArch enabled at a regular interval. This ensures images stay up-to-date with the latest code changes.
dockerRebuild.manualRebuildDescription: Immediately trigger a rebuild of all Docker images for every subresource with ReArch enabled. Each subresource will be queued as a separate job that you can monitor in the Jobs section.
dockerRebuild.failedToLoadSettings: Failed to load docker rebuild settings: {{message}}
dockerRebuild.failedToSaveSettings: Failed to save docker rebuild settings: {{message}}
dockerRebuild.rebuildAllConfirmMessage: This will rebuild all Docker images for every subresource with ReArch enabled. Continue?

containerCleanup.title: Container Cleanup
containerCleanup.noRearchEnabledDescription: Container cleanup is only relevant when at least one subresource has the ReArch integration enabled. Enable ReArch on a repository's settings to use this feature.
containerCleanup.scheduledCleanupDescription: When enabled, the system periodically checks for idle containers and applies two thresholds: first containers are stopped (freeing compute but keeping the container on disk), then after a longer period they are removed entirely. Conversation data is always preserved.
containerCleanup.stopIdleContainersDescription: Running containers with no user interaction for this long will be stopped. The container is preserved on disk.
containerCleanup.removeStoppedContainersDescription: Stopped containers that remain idle for this long will be permanently removed from Docker. The conversation is kept.
containerCleanup.manualCleanupDescription: Immediately run both cleanup phases: stop idle running containers and remove stale stopped containers based on the configured thresholds. If no thresholds are configured, defaults of 30 minutes (stop) and 1 day (remove) are used.
containerCleanup.failedToLoadSettings: Failed to load container cleanup settings: {{message}}
containerCleanup.failedToSaveSettings: Failed to save container cleanup settings: {{message}}
containerCleanup.triggerCleanupMessage: This will immediately stop idle running containers and remove stale stopped containers. Continue?
containerCleanup.cleanupCompleteNoIdle: Cleanup complete: no idle containers found.
containerCleanup.stopThresholdError: Stop threshold must be between {{min}} and {{max}} {{unit}}.
containerCleanup.removeThresholdError: Remove threshold must be between {{min}} and {{max}} {{unit}}.

bitbucketImport.searchDescription: Search for Bitbucket repositories to import

bitbucketResourceForm.connectDescription: Connect to Bitbucket workspaces and repositories to bring your code activity into context.
bitbucketResourceForm.name: Name
bitbucketResourceForm.nameError: Name must be between 2 and 100 characters
bitbucketResourceForm.howToGetCredentials: How to get your Bitbucket credentials
bitbucketResourceForm.helpWorkspaceStep1: Log in to Bitbucket in your browser.
bitbucketResourceForm.helpWorkspaceStep2: Click on your workspace name in the top navigation.
bitbucketResourceForm.helpWorkspaceStep3: The workspace slug is visible in the URL:
bitbucketResourceForm.helpEmailDescription: Use the email address associated with your Atlassian/Bitbucket account.
bitbucketResourceForm.helpCloneUsernameStep2: Your username is displayed under "Bitbucket profile settings". This is the username used for HTTPS clone operations.
bitbucketResourceForm.helpApiTokenStep7: Copy the generated token and paste it into the field above.
bitbucketResourceForm.failedToSave: Failed to save resource. Please try again.

bitbucketRepoDetails.enableRearchDescription: Allow this repository to be used as a ReArch environment
bitbucketRepoDetails.noServicesConfigured: No services configured. Click a button above to add one.
bitbucketRepoDetails.skillsRepositoriesDescription: Select repositories containing skills specific to this repository. These are cloned alongside default skills when a conversation starts.
bitbucketRepoDetails.resourceConstraintsDescription: Set Docker resource limits for containers created from this repository. Leave at 0 for no limit.
bitbucketRepoDetails.noBranchSelected: No branch selected. Click the edit button to select one.
bitbucketRepoDetails.helpGuideDescription: Not sure which to pick? Read the docs
bitbucketRepoDetails.templateBuiltIn: A built-in Dockerfile will be used. The repository does not need a .rearch/ folder.
bitbucketRepoDetails.templateCustom: The repository must contain a .rearch/ folder with a Dockerfile.
bitbucketRepoDetails.deleteSubresourceConfirm: Delete this subresource? This action cannot be undone.
bitbucketRepoDetails.syncStarted: Sync process started successfully! Job ID: {{jobId}}
bitbucketRepoDetails.dockerBuildStarted: Docker build started successfully! Job ID: {{jobId}}
bitbucketRepoDetails.noExposeLinesFound: No EXPOSE lines found in .rearch/Dockerfile. Services were not changed.
bitbucketRepoDetails.noDockerfileFound: No .rearch/Dockerfile found in the repository. Services were not changed.
bitbucketRepoDetails.portAlreadyInUse: Port {{port}} is already in use by another service.

githubResourceForm.nameError: Name must be between 2 and 100 characters.
githubResourceForm.privateKeyRequired: GitHub App private key is required.
githubResourceForm.installationIdRequired: GitHub App installation ID is required.
githubResourceForm.failedToSave: Failed to save resource. Please try again.
githubResourceForm.connectDescription: Connect to GitHub repositories using a GitHub App to bring your code into context.
githubResourceForm.name: Resource Name
githubResourceForm.privateKeyPlaceholder:
-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
...
-----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
githubResourceForm.helpAppIdStep1: Go to your GitHub Settings > Developer settings > GitHub Apps.
githubResourceForm.helpAppIdStep2: Select your GitHub App (or create a new one).
githubResourceForm.helpAppIdStep3: The App ID is displayed on the app's General settings page.
githubResourceForm.helpPrivateKeyStep1: On your GitHub App's settings page, scroll to "Private keys".
githubResourceForm.helpPrivateKeyStep2: Click "Generate a private key" to download a .pem file.
githubResourceForm.helpPrivateKeyStep3: Upload the downloaded .pem file using the button above.
githubResourceForm.helpInstallationIdStep1: Install the GitHub App on your organization or account.
githubResourceForm.helpInstallationIdStep2: Go to Settings > Applications > Installed GitHub Apps.
githubResourceForm.helpInstallationIdStep3: Click "Configure" next to your app — the Installation ID is the number in the URL.

githubImport.searchDescription: Search for repositories accessible to this GitHub App installation.
githubImport.noRepositoriesFound: No repositories found matching your search.
